channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Austin endorses women in combat jobs and exhorts West Point cadets to defend the Constitution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin used a speech at West Point Wednesday to forcefully endorse having women in combat roles and emphasize the military's obligation to defend the U.S. Constitution — ideals some fear may come under fire in the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: “I am pleased to announce that former Congressman Billy Long, of the Great State of Missouri, will be appointed to serve as the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS),” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Unlikely battleground California plays key role -- again -- in setting US House's political balance
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Democrats and Republicans agreed on at least one thing before November’s election: California would play a central role in determining control of the U.S. House. Indeed it did. Democrat Adam Gray's come-from-behind victory over Republican Rep.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Gunning for the 2nd Amendment
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps Gail Slater to lead DOJ antitrust division
item.description: Gail Slater will take over the DOJ's high-profile antitrust cases against tech giants like Google and Apple.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Sotomayor compares trans medical 'treatments' to aspirin in question about side effects during oral arguments
item.description: Justice Sonia Sotomayor said "every medical treatment has a risk, even taking aspirin" during oral arguments in the high-profile SCOTUS case involving trans "treatments" for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump selects a former soldier and Iraq War veteran to serve as Army secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Wednesday that he has selected a former soldier and Iraq War veteran to serve as secretary of the Army. Trump said Daniel P. Driscoll had completed Army Ranger school and deployed with the 10th Mountain Division to Iraq.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems renew push for limiting presidential clemency powers after Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: Calls to limit presidential pardon powers have fallen on party lines in the House of Representatives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's hush money dismissal motion invokes Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: President-elect Trump’s attorneys laid out their arguments for why his criminal conviction in New York should be dismissed entirely in a new court filing made public Tuesday in which they cited Hunter Biden’s pardon earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Migrants nervous over Trump plans for mass deportations
item.description: "I am traumatized. If I don't get the appointment (with border officials) I will go back," one woman said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota budget update shows $1.1B less than prior forecast
item.description: The economic report released Wednesday morning by state budget officials projects a downturn, with only a $616 million cushion expected by the end of the next budget period. The new data will guide lawmakers when budget deliberations begin in January.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Judge in Hunter Biden tax case rebukes president’s claims of unfair treatment
item.description: The federal judge overseeing Hunter Biden’s tax case issued a sharp rebuke of President Biden’s claims his son was unfairly prosecuted in new court filings Tuesday night. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House panel votes to absorb legal fees of DFLer in employment dispute
item.description: A DFL state lawmaker will have legal fees covered by the Minnesota House after alleging her rights were violated by an outside employer. Republicans say the move sets a bad precedent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: Other than Bluesky’s own account, Ocasio-Cortez’s follower landmark is a first for a user, the platform told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions have scored a major legal victory with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California bill would allow public university admission priority for slaves' descendants
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — A California lawmaker introduced a bill Monday to allow admission priority to the descendants of slaves at the University of California and California State University, two of the largest public university systems in the nation.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, TRÂN NGUYỄN and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: Already reeling from their November defeat at the polls, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden's pardoning of his son for a federal felony conviction — after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who operates above the law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: On the Record: NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City has a new police commissioner. Jessica Tisch is now the second woman to lead the nation’s largest police department.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
